text:
Once upon a time, there was an adventurous goose named Gosforth.

Gosforth loved exploring. He soared across meadows, flew over forests, and glided down valleys. One crisp autumn morning, Gosforth flew southward, craving something different.

He spotted a vast stretch of sky unoccupied by clouds. Gosforth decided to dive in. After drifting aimlessly for what felt like hours, he realized he was close to something mysterious. Emerging into what felt like enchanted mist, he realized his wings were slick.

Inquisitive, Gosforth fluttered closer. Blades glimmered underneath. They felt soft!

A beautiful bird with feathers as violet as ancient ivies took flight. It was an eagle—an old soul. The eagle circled Gosforth with a compassionate gaze.

“Lost?”

Gosforth nodded. “Yes.”

“You crave adventure.”

“But sometimes...”

The eagle leaned closer. “Just enjoy the flight. Learn. Explore. And maybe... just maybe once?”

Gos
